citations.narrative:
An outside run of an enclosure from the sheltered housing facility containing three 5-month-old female Labrador dogs has
a portion of the wall that is not impervious to moisture. The dogs have chewed away an area, approximately 1.5 ft in
length and 7 inches at the highest point, of the vinyl paneling located just above the cement partition of the wall along the
building. The area underneath the missing vinyl is plyboard. Areas that dogs come into contact within a sheltered housing
facility must be impervious to moisture so that appropriate cleaning and sanitizing can be performed. Correct by ensuring
all walls and other surfaces in contact with the animals be impervious to moisture. Correct by: March 1, 2026.

Three 5-month-old female Labrador dogs did not have access to water. When asked, the licensee refilled their bowl of
water. All of the dogs drank from the bowl, from approximately 45 seconds to 90 seconds in duration. Two started initially
drinking greedily for approximately 20 and 45 seconds. The dog that drank for 20 seconds came back to the bowl again
and drank more. The third dog came in when they cleared out and drank for a few seconds.
Two 4-month-old Cane Corso dogs also had no access to water in their enclosure. Their water bowl was empty and when
it was filled, they did not drink and did not show interest in the water.
The licensee reports the water bowls were filled this morning during chores.
Access to water is necessary to prevent excessive thirst or dehydration. Correct by ensuring potable water is provided
continually to all dogs and puppies.
Correct by: January 22, 2026, onward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
3.3(e)(1)(iii) 
   
Sheltered housing facilities. 
An outside run of an enclosure from the sheltered housing facility containing three 5-month-old female Labrador dogs has 
a portion of the wall that is not impervious to moisture. The dogs have chewed away an area, approximately 1.5 ft in 
length and 7 inches at the highest point, of the vinyl paneling located just above the cement partition of the wall along the 
building. The area underneath the missing vinyl is plyboard. Areas that dogs come into contact within a sheltered housing 
facility must be impervious to moisture so that appropriate cleaning and sanitizing can be performed. Correct by ensuring 
all walls and other surfaces in contact with the animals be impervious to moisture. Correct by: March 1, 2026. 
3.10(a)  Direct
   
Watering 
Three 5-month-old female Labrador dogs did not have access to water. When asked, the licensee refilled their bowl of 
water. All of the dogs drank from the bowl, from approximately 45 seconds to 90 seconds in duration. Two started initially 
drinking greedily for approximately 20 and 45 seconds. The dog that drank for 20 seconds came back to the bowl again 
and drank more. The third dog came in when they cleared out and drank for a few seconds.  
 
Two 4-month-old Cane Corso dogs also had no access to water in their enclosure. Their water bowl was empty and when 
it was filled, they did not drink and did not show interest in the water.  
 
The licensee reports the water bowls were filled this morning during chores.  
 
Access to water is necessary to prevent excessive thirst or dehydration. Correct by ensuring potable water is provided 
continually to all dogs and puppies.  
 
Correct by: January 22, 2026, onward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
